candidates.stmt:
I am a Qualified Accountant with over 40 years business and industry experience. I am committed to cutting and eliminating all waste to deliver the best value for money for your Council tax. I commit to be a strong voice being accessible and responsive not ignoring requests for help as many experience now. We at Reform oppose rip off Car park charges which hurt our local retailers. We will work hard with the police to fight shoplifting and all anti social behaviour.
I have knocked on over 700 doors so far and will continue to do so until election day listening to the issues concerning you. Have the other parties bothered to talk to you ??
 Vote for change on May 7th vote for  Dave Lovell Reform Uk

Fleet is a fantastic town, but residents increasingly feel infrastructure and services are struggling to keep up with change. I have lived in Fleet for 24 years and have been part of the local area for over 50 years.
Professionally I work in the technology sector leading international teams and managing risk in critical environments. I believe local government should focus on practical decisions that protect residents, support local businesses, and maintain the quality of life that makes Fleet such a great place to live for everyone in our community.
I live locally with my family and am committed to protecting Fleet’s character for future generations.

Protect Fleet’s Character
Fleet has seen significant housing growth over the past decade. Future development must only proceed when infrastructure, roads, schools and GP services can properly support it, so that our town remains a great place to live.

Fix Roads and Infrastructure
Residents have seen a noticeable decline in the condition of local roads and pavements over the past year. I will press Hampshire County Council to prioritise repairs and ensure Fleet receives its fair share of infrastructure investment.

Revitalise Fleet Town Centre
Fleet deserves a thriving town centre. I want to support and attract independent shops, restaurants and local businesses that make the High Street somewhere residents enjoy visiting again and that better serves the needs of our residents.

Many of our residents in Fleet are struggling to get by. Rising energy, food, and fuel prices have left people working harder and paying more, but getting less. Services desperately need improving. We need a local voice with strong values to make sure our Council steps up. Vote for Andrew Perkins

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

candidates.stmt:
Dan Taylor has proudly called Fleet home for over 15 years, raising his family in a town he cares deeply about. A committed community leader, he serves on both Fleet Town Council and Hart District Council, working to protect what makes the area special while supporting its future growth. Dan is a strong advocate for young people, helping deliver projects such as the skate park at The Views and supporting Scouting locally as a Trustee and Treasurer. He is also passionate about a thriving town centre, playing an active role in Fleet’s Saturday Market and contributing to plans for the Harlington’s future as a key community hub.

Alongside this, Dan champions health, wellbeing and the environment through practical action – from supporting mental health initiatives to volunteering at local events and caring for Fleet’s green spaces. He takes a hands-on approach to planning, ensuring development is carefully considered, sustainable and in line with the town’s long-term needs. Above all, Dan is driven by a commitment to community spirit, strong local services and preserving Fleet as a vibrant, welcoming place to live

I am standing in Fleet East because I believe residents deserve a councillor who listens and stands alongside the community.

I have first-hand experience advocating for elderly relatives and for my daughter’s special educational needs.

I will bring that same determination to working for the people of Fleet East.

I have lived in the area for over 20 years and served as a Parish Councillor for the past decade, working in the best interests of our community.

Being self-employed, I understand hard work and practical decision-making. I have seen traffic increase and access to GP appointments decline due to poor planning.

I want to be a strong voice for residents, focused on sensible development, better local services, and protecting our quality of life.

**I am a local volunteer and have been chosen as Hart Lib Dems’ candidate for Hampshire County Council’s Odiham & Hook Division, and Hart District Council’s Hook Ward**.

I and my husband Tim have lived in Hook for over 30 years, and our son attended Hook and Robert Mays schools. I have experienced both the vibrant and supportive nature of the local community, as well as its increase in its size.

Our daughter Lizzie was born in 2000 with a rare genetic condition, and the 26 years since have been a huge learning curve for me in the SEND (Special Educational Needs and Disabilities) world.

In 2004, I became a parent governor of Lizzie’s SEND school, serving as Chair of Governors between 2016 and 2021, before finally retiring as a governor last year.

I also signed up to serve as a Parent Representative with Hampshire Parent Carer Network, a support forum and service provider for parents in similar circumstances.

Notably, Lizzie’s disability encouraged me to become a Parent Trustee and fundraiser with local charity Disability Challengers. Fundraising for Disability Challengers, as well as for national charity SeeAbility, has seen me run the London marathon three times, with fantastic support from Hook Runners, of which I has been a member since 2016.

My love of the outdoors is also a key contributor to my desire to make the countryside more accessible for all, with its known benefits to mental health and wellbeing.

I am also a major advocate of sustainability, and I am eager to contribute to Lib Dem-led Hart’s nationally-recognised work in this area. The District’s aims align well with my qualifications in art and design, which emphasise creative solutions, sustainability and up-cycling.

** What would my priorities be as a Councillor,?** “Our Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) system is in crisis, and this must be addressed as soon as possible.

“Fortunately, we have a brilliant local MP, Alex Brewer, who similarly has a background working with chil
Just a local person who wants to see the best for people in Hampshire.

I was brought up on the Hearsey Gardens estate in Blackwater. My family moved there in 1970 and my mum is still there today.
I attended Frogmore Infants, Potley Hill Juniors and on occasion Frogmore Comprehensive school. I’ve lived in Essex for the last 20 years, where I was active in the local Labour Party.

When I returned to my home town a year ago. I decided that the Labour Party no longer reflected my views on the environment, fair taxation, attitudes to the far-right and the failure to tackle the cost of living crisis.

I’m a small business owner, working in fire protection. Outside of work I enjoy long cycle rides and following a once great London based football club. I’m looking forward to assisting the Green Party in growing into a real alternative to the failed politics of the last 50 years.

Trevor Lloyd-Jones went to school and grew up in Yateley in Northeast Hampshire from childhood. Former school governor specialising in data, education KPIs and performance, he has experience managing small and very large businesses with a lifetime career in publishing, marketing, and the digital and technology industries. Former Conservative and former Conservative candidate Trevor is standing on a true reform agenda and a ‘small c’ conservative agenda: reform of the NHS, the judiciary, tax, with a pro-growth economic plan, and a new deal for young people on student debt and on home ownership.

Trevor is campaigning against cuts to the armed forces, the hollowing out of Britain’s defence sector and this government’s shoddy treatment of veterans. He was one of the Brexit Party candidates who stood aside for the Boris Johnson intake of Conservatives at the 2019 election.

In the media and locally Trevor is lobbying passionately for a new kind of bottom-up/bubble-up politics, an open, more ambitious and accessible politics. He speaks out against undemocratic and faceless global organisations taking the place of our democratic institutions.

But now, after more than a decade of the Conservatives the public have become conditioned to accept failure. They’ve become used to ministers blaming everyone but themselves, whether it’s the House of Lords, immigration lawyers, EU bureaucrats or the European Court of Human Rights. In the media and locally Trevor is lobbying passionately for a new kind of bottom-up/bubble-up politics, an open, more ambitious and accessible politics. He speaks out against undemocratic and faceless global organisations taking the place of our democratic institutions.

When not on the campaign trail Trevor can be found enjoying countryside walks or writing books on philosophy, politics and 'conscious capitalism'. He holds a degree in Economics and Public Administration from University of London and a Masters degree in Business Administration.

Lisa, who has proudly called Yateley home for well over 20 years, and the local area for over 40 years, is standing as your Conservative candidate for Yateley West on 7 May.

A proven community champion, as founder and chair of Back Off Berkeley Homes she continues to lead opposition to Firgrove Farm and other green gap developments affecting Yateley. She will also prioritise local services and tackling crime and anti-social behaviour.

Lisa is known locally for her active campaigning against the controversial planning application for Firgrove Farm, as well as other inappropriate local green gap development.  She is the Founder, and Chair, of the Back Off Berkeley Homes action group, working closely with other community organisations to fight this and other local planning issues.
  
Lisa currently works within education as a key worker.  She is also a registered therapist.  Prior to this, Lisa was an international corporate communications specialist working both within industry and with her own consultancy.  During this time, she worked closely with both national and international governments and politicians, including working within challenging geopolitical environments.
 
 Lisa is passionate about protecting our local green spaces, wildlife and environment.  Her priorities are fighting to retain the character of Yateley West, not just for our generation, but for many more  to come.  Lisa is also hoping to tackle, alongside local agencies, the anti-social behaviour that can blight the lives of local residents.  This includes intimidating behaviour within our town centre, local streets and recreation areas, as well as wildlife crime, and unlicenced vehicles disturbing residents use of common land.
 
 Lisa looks to bring a new perspective to representing the community that Yateley appears to have lost under LibDem control.  She plans to bring much more active public representation for constituents.  She is here to listen to, and represent, what local residents want m
